prose:
**Formaldehyde frontier-orbital analysis** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) in **PySCF**, **gas phase** (no solvent model), **charge 0, multiplicity 1** (closed-shell RKS). Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` (`/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz`, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) used as-is — this was a single point at the provided geometry, not re-optimized. SCF converged in 8 cycles. Note: chemkit uses density fitting (RI) by default for the two-electron integrals.

**Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8697 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

Supporting frontier-orbital values (eV):
- HOMO (index 7) = −7.6523
- LUMO (index 8) = −1.7826
- HOMO−1 = −11.0025, HOMO−2 = −12.4869
- LUMO+1 = +1.3920, LUMO+2 = +2.8397

Koopmans-derived descriptors (eV): vertical IP = 7.6523, vertical EA = 1.7826, electronegativity = 4.7175, chemical hardness = 2.9348, chemical softness = 0.34073 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 3.7914.

Total (electronic) energy: −114.55325 Hartree (= −3117.1528 eV = −71883.251 kcal/mol); energy zero = electronic energy (bare nuclei + electrons). Note these absolute energies are method-specific and only comparable to same-method/same-basis values.

**Convergence & integrity:** SCF converged = true; engine integrity status = "ok", trustworthy = true. **No warnings were reported** by the engine (the result JSON contained no `warnings` array / entries).

**Reproducibility:** command — `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpc9gz3o51/live.json`. The result JSON was written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpc9gz3o51/live.json`. (No literature/experimental comparison is offered — ask if you want one and I will verify a source first.)
